# UC-18.2.8 · NSX Edge Gateway Health

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Fault, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We guard the software edge routers so North-South paths cannot pretend to be fine while BGP, NAT, load sharing, or VPN pieces quietly struggle before regular folks only notice dead apps.*

---

## Description

Fuses **VMware NSX-T** **Policy** **gateway** **state** (**`vmware:nsx:gateway_health`**) with **transport-node** **status** rows (**`vmware:nsx:edge_status`**) so **Tier-0/Tier-1** **service** **routers** reveal **BGP**, **NAT**, **LB**, and **VPN** **posture** per **logical** **router** before **north-south** sessions brown out.

## Value

Customers lose **outbound** **SaaS**, **partner** **VPNs**, and **load-balanced** **ingress** when **Edge** **SR** **services** look **green** in **high-level** **NOC** tiles but **individual** **state** fields already scream **degraded**; **one** **Splunk** **row** tying **`lr_latest`**, **`fault_class`**, **recent** **audit** actors, and **vCenter** **motion** keeps **VMware**, **security**, and **network** teams from **burning** **hours** deciding whether the fault is **policy**, **datapath**, or **maintenance**-**induced**.

## Implementation

Enable **HTTPS** collectors for **`/policy/api/v1/infra/tier-0s/{id}/state`** and **`/api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/status`** mirrors; normalize **`overall_status`**, **`bgp_status`**, **`nat_status`**, **`lb_status`**, **`vpn_status`**, **`lr_name`**, **`edge_node_id`**; ship **`vmware:nsx:transport_node`**, **`vmware:nsx:audit`**, **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** sidecars; **map** **MoIDs** through **`vcenter_edge_host_map.csv`**; schedule **`uc_18_2_8_nsx_edge_gateway_health`** every **5m** with **`nsx_edge_change_window.csv`** throttles; publish **`ds_nsx_edge_gateway`**.

## Evidence

Saved search **`uc_18_2_8_nsx_edge_gateway_health`**, optional **Dashboard Studio** workbook **`ds_nsx_edge_gateway`** with **`tok_edge_cluster`**, **`tok_lr`**, **`tok_fault_class`**, and weekly CSV exports of `fault_class` by `edge_key`.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
- **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) wherever `vmware:nsx:gateway_health`, `vmware:nsx:edge_status`, `vmware:nsx:transport_node`, and `vmware:nsx:audit` parsing runs for VMware NSX 4.2 / 4.1 / NSX-T 3.2.x per Broadcom lifecycle pages.
- **vCenter context** — Add **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) so `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` captures maintenance, vMotion, and evacuation tasks tied to Edge VM MoIDs (map MoID↔`transport_node_id` with `vcenter_edge_host_map.csv`).
- **Syslog scale** — Terminate high-volume Edge `/var/log` feeds on **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with precise `TIME_PREFIX` for ISO-8601 timestamps.
- **ACI hybrid (optional)** — Install **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) when Tier-0 handoffs land on ACI L3Out SVIs; use `cisco:aci:syslog` as corroboration, not a substitute NSX signal. Sample fabric dashboards may reference **Cisco Data Center Networking** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)).
- **RBAC & TLS** — Collector accounts remain read-only for Policy `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/tier-0s/{id}/state`, Manager `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/status`, locale-service SR health JSON, BGP/NAT/LB/VPN status children, and audit categories; avoid TLS MITM on Manager/Policy 443 that breaks client certificates; honor HTTP 429 with jittered backoff.
- **Lookup hygiene** — Maintain `vcenter_edge_host_map.csv` and `nsx_edge_change_window.csv` (`lr_name`, `start_epoch`, `end_epoch`, `chg_id`) before any paging macros fire.

**Throughput planning** — Budget **6–18 KB/min** per **edge cluster** when **`gateway_health`** and **`edge_status`** **poll** together; keep **`JSON`** indexes **lean** and **`syslog`** archives **separate**.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) **`vmware:nsx:gateway_health`** — HTTPS workers every 60–120s call `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/tier-0s/{id}/state` and Tier-1 mirrors your operations guide names; emit `overall_status`, `health`, `gateway_state`, `bgp_status`, `nat_status`, `lb_status`, `vpn_status`, `ipsec_state`, `logical_router_id`, `lr_name`, `edge_node_id`, `message`, `detail`.
(2) **`vmware:nsx:edge_status`** — Poll `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/status` (plus inter-site JSON when stretched Tier-0 applies) so datapath advisories accompany abstract policy state; capture `edge_node`, `transport_node_id`, uplink/service messages the TA documents.
(3) **`vmware:nsx:transport_node`** — Scheduled `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes` with `admin_state`, `maintenance_mode`, `cluster_name`, `node_type`, `id` for maintenance joins.
(4) **`vmware:nsx:audit`** — Ship audit/API trails when `request_uri` touches `tier-0`, `tier-1`, `locale-service`, `edge-cluster`, `bgp`, `nat`, load-balancer, or VPN publishes.
(5) **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** — Include tasks or events that show ESXi maintenance when Edge VMs evacuate; enrich with `vcenter_edge_host_map.csv` so join keys align with NSX IDs.

**Step 2 — Create the search**
Promote the SPL into macro **`uc_18_2_8_nsx_edge_gateway_health`** when index aliases stabilize, then iterate thresholds inside `macros.conf` rather than inline.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.8 NSX Edge Tier-0/Tier-1 SR health")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:gateway_health" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(edge_node_id,transport_node_id,edge_node,"")))
| eval lr_key=trim(lower(coalesce(logical_router_id,lr_name,tier0_id,tier1_id,"")))
| eval health_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(overall_status,health,gateway_state,"")))
| eval bgp_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(bgp_status,bgp_neighbor_state,"")))
| eval nat_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(nat_status,"")))
| eval lb_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(lb_status,loadbalancer_health,"")))
| eval vpn_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(vpn_status,ipsec_state,"")))
| eval diag=lower(coalesce(message,detail,_raw,""))
| eval svc_degraded=if(health_raw!="" AND NOT match(health_raw,"^(UP|HEALTHY|OK|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval bgp_degraded=if(bgp_raw IN ("IDLE","ACTIVE","CONNECT","OPENSENT","OPENCONFIRM","DOWN","FAILED"),1,if(bgp_raw!="" AND bgp_raw!="ESTABLISHED" AND match(diag,"(?i)bgp|peer|tier-0"),1,0))
| eval nat_degraded=if(nat_raw!="" AND NOT match(nat_raw,"^(UP|OK|ENABLED|HEALTHY|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval lb_degraded=if(lb_raw!="" AND NOT match(lb_raw,"^(UP|OK|ENABLED|HEALTHY|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval vpn_degraded=if(vpn_raw!="" AND NOT match(vpn_raw,"^(UP|ESTABLISHED|TUNNEL_UP)$"),1,0)
| eval fault_class=case(svc_degraded==1,"gateway_health_not_green",bgp_degraded==1,"bgp_not_established_like",nat_degraded==1,"nat_service_stressed",lb_degraded==1,"lb_service_stressed",vpn_degraded==1,"vpn_service_stressed",null())
| where isnotnull(fault_class)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(health_raw) as health_latest latest(bgp_raw) as bgp_latest latest(nat_raw) as nat_latest latest(lb_raw) as lb_latest latest(vpn_raw) as vpn_latest latest(lr_key) as lr_latest max(_time) as last_seen by edge_key fault_class
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:transport_node" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(id,transport_node_id,""))) | eval mm=if(match(lower(coalesce(admin_state,maintenance_mode,_raw)),"(?i)maintenance|quarantine"),1,0) | stats max(mm) as tn_mm values(cluster_name) as edge_cluster by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | where match(lower(coalesce(request_uri,resource_path,_raw)),"(?i)tier-0|edge-cluster|gateway|bgp|nat|vpn") | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(target_transport_node,resource_id,""))) | stats latest(_time) as audit_ts latest(user_name) as audit_actor by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|evacuat|vmotion") | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(host_mo_id,host_id,""))) | stats latest(_time) as vc_ts max(if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance"),1,0)) as vc_mm by edge_key ]
| eval severity=case(fault_class="gateway_health_not_green",95,fault_class="bgp_not_established_like",90,fault_class="vpn_service_stressed",85,fault_class="lb_service_stressed",80,fault_class="nat_service_stressed",75,1==1,50)
| eval suppress=if(tn_mm==1 OR vc_mm==1 OR audit_ts>relative_time(last_seen,"-12m"),1,0)
| where suppress==0
| sort - severity - last_seen
| head 200
| table last_seen severity edge_key lr_latest fault_class health_latest bgp_latest nat_latest lb_latest vpn_latest edge_cluster audit_ts audit_actor vc_ts
```

**Understanding this SPL**
- **Fault classes** — `gateway_health_not_green` surfaces composite health that is not UP/HEALTHY/RUNNING; `bgp_not_established_like` catches non-ESTABLISHED BGP enums or BGP-flavored tokens in `diag`; `nat_service_stressed`, `lb_service_stressed`, `vpn_service_stressed` isolate SR-attached services so vague DOWN labels do not hide root cause.
- **Normalization** — `upper`/`trim` on raw vendor strings prevents `up`/`UP` splits in stats.
- **Deduplication** — `bin` + `stats` collapse five-minute bursts so flapping polls do not spam results; tune span per poll cadence.
- **Maintenance enrichment** — `transport_node` join stamps `tn_mm`; `vmware:nsx:audit` shows who published Tier-0/Tier-1 changes minutes before symptoms; `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` hints host maintenance (MoID joins may require CSV glue—verify joins before paging).
- **Suppression logic** — `suppress` drops rows when transport nodes are in maintenance, vCenter hints at MM, or audit touches land within twelve minutes of `last_seen`; adjust lag to match change discipline.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) **UI parity** — NSX Manager/Policy Tier-0/Tier-1 SR health screens should mirror `health_latest`, `bgp_latest`, `nat_latest`, `lb_latest`, `vpn_latest` for the same `lr_latest`.
(2) **CLI spot-check** — On Edge nodes, `get` style commands your runbook lists for BGP neighbors, NAT tables, LB virtual servers, and IPsec SAs must agree with `fault_class`; fix `props`/`transforms` aliases if not.
(3) **fieldsummary hygiene** — After TA upgrades run `fieldsummary` on `vmware:nsx:gateway_health` for `overall_status`, `bgp_status`, `edge_node_id`, `lr_name`; repeat for `vmware:nsx:edge_status` message tokens referenced in `diag`.
(4) **Cardinality guard** — `dc(edge_key)` for fault rows should stay near installed physical edge count; multiples imply duplicate pollers or stale IDs.
(5) **Join truth** — If `vc_ts` never populates, refresh `vcenter_edge_host_map.csv`; blank MoID joins are expected until the CSV lands.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
(1) **Schedule** — Saved search every five minutes writing to summary index `summary_nsx_edge_gw` (optional) for leadership rollups.
(2) **Ticketing** — Push `edge_key`, `lr_latest`, `fault_class`, `health_latest`, `bgp_latest` into ITSM custom fields; attach NSX UI deep links from `lookup_nsx_console.csv`.
(3) **Data quality** — Alert when `vmware:nsx:gateway_health` goes silent >15m for production clusters—stale happy data hurts more than noisy polls.
(4) **Ownership** — Restrict macro edits to `splunk_role_nsx_edge_platform`; App teams get filtered dashboards with LB/VPN tokens only when they own those services.
(5) **ES correlation** — Optional Enterprise Security notables may wrap CIM Alerts; keep raw observability panels for engineering drill-down.

**Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
**Visualization guidance** — Dashboard Studio `ds_nsx_edge_gateway`: Row A single-values for counts of `gateway_health_not_green`, `bgp_not_established_like`, `nat_service_stressed`, `lb_service_stressed`, `vpn_service_stressed` with tokens `tok_edge_cluster`, `tok_lr`, `tok_fault_class`. Row B matrix of `edge_key` × `fault_class` colored by `severity` with tooltips showing `health_latest`/`bgp_latest`. Row C timeline stacking `fault_class` by `_time`. Row D detail table for `audit_actor`, `audit_ts`, `edge_cluster`, `vc_ts` so operators see who touched Policy before symptoms. Optional ACI strip charts when Splunkbase 4022 feeds exist—keep NSX API tiles authoritative.
**Alert design** — `ALRT_NSX_GW_HARD`: `fault_class=gateway_health_not_green`, `severity>=95`, throttle 15m per `edge_key`+`lr_latest`, require two polls. `ALRT_NSX_GW_BGP`: `bgp_not_established_like`, throttle 10m, attach carrier circuit IDs from `lookup_wan_circuit.csv`. `ALRT_NSX_GW_SVC`: NAT/LB/VPN classes, throttle 30m, suppress when `tn_mm=1` or `inputlookup nsx_edge_change_window` matches `lr_latest`.
**Troubleshooting** — SR CPU starvation can look like service DOWN—pair UC-18.2.4. VIP moves can lag realization (`policy_realization_lag`) or stale pages (`api_partial_state_page`); honor `cursor`. Drills trip BGP rows (`planned_sr_failover_drill`); vMotion blips (`edge_vmotion_blip`); labs (`single_uplink_lab_noise`); GR (`graceful_restart_bgp_hold`); NAT churn (`nat_rule_publish_shadow`).
**Governance** — Quarterly `fault_class` vs CRQ; log collector gaps; rehearse Dashboard Studio → CLI → carrier.

**Instrumentation checklist** — After TA upgrades run `fieldsummary` on `vmware:nsx:gateway_health` for **`overall_status`**, **`bgp_status`**, **`edge_node_id`**, **`lr_name`**; confirm `vmware:nsx:edge_status` still extracts **`message`**; verify `vmware:nsx:transport_node` exposes **`admin_state`**, **`maintenance_mode`**; ensure audit retains **`request_uri`**, **`user_name`**; validate vCenter **`_time`** skew <1 poll; monitor derived **`fault_class`**, **`severity`**, **`suppress`**, **`tn_mm`**, **`audit_ts`**, **`vc_ts`**, **`svc_degraded`**, **`bgp_degraded`**, **`nat_degraded`**, **`lb_degraded`**, **`vpn_degraded`**, **`vc_mm`**, **`diag`**, **`lr_latest`**, **`health_latest`**, **`bgp_latest`** in saved-search tests.

**Glossary** — **SR** means Tier-0/Tier-1 service router dataplane on Edge nodes coordinating BGP, NAT, LB, and VPN services. **Policy path** `/infra/tier-0s/{id}/state` is the declarative health object this UC tracks beside transport-node status.

**Sibling handoff** — Pair **UC-18.2.4** (CPU), **UC-18.2.12** (SR HA), **UC-18.2.16** (BFD/uplink vs BGP); tunnel-only issues stay **UC-18.2.9**.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.8 NSX Edge Tier-0/Tier-1 SR health")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:gateway_health" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(edge_node_id,transport_node_id,edge_node,"")))
| eval lr_key=trim(lower(coalesce(logical_router_id,lr_name,tier0_id,tier1_id,"")))
| eval health_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(overall_status,health,gateway_state,"")))
| eval bgp_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(bgp_status,bgp_neighbor_state,"")))
| eval nat_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(nat_status,"")))
| eval lb_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(lb_status,loadbalancer_health,"")))
| eval vpn_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(vpn_status,ipsec_state,"")))
| eval diag=lower(coalesce(message,detail,_raw,""))
| eval svc_degraded=if(health_raw!="" AND NOT match(health_raw,"^(UP|HEALTHY|OK|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval bgp_degraded=if(bgp_raw IN ("IDLE","ACTIVE","CONNECT","OPENSENT","OPENCONFIRM","DOWN","FAILED"),1,if(bgp_raw!="" AND bgp_raw!="ESTABLISHED" AND match(diag,"(?i)bgp|peer|tier-0"),1,0))
| eval nat_degraded=if(nat_raw!="" AND NOT match(nat_raw,"^(UP|OK|ENABLED|HEALTHY|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval lb_degraded=if(lb_raw!="" AND NOT match(lb_raw,"^(UP|OK|ENABLED|HEALTHY|RUNNING)$"),1,0)
| eval vpn_degraded=if(vpn_raw!="" AND NOT match(vpn_raw,"^(UP|ESTABLISHED|TUNNEL_UP)$"),1,0)
| eval fault_class=case(svc_degraded==1,"gateway_health_not_green",bgp_degraded==1,"bgp_not_established_like",nat_degraded==1,"nat_service_stressed",lb_degraded==1,"lb_service_stressed",vpn_degraded==1,"vpn_service_stressed",null())
| where isnotnull(fault_class)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(health_raw) as health_latest latest(bgp_raw) as bgp_latest latest(nat_raw) as nat_latest latest(lb_raw) as lb_latest latest(vpn_raw) as vpn_latest latest(lr_key) as lr_latest max(_time) as last_seen by edge_key fault_class
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:transport_node" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(id,transport_node_id,""))) | eval mm=if(match(lower(coalesce(admin_state,maintenance_mode,_raw)),"(?i)maintenance|quarantine"),1,0) | stats max(mm) as tn_mm values(cluster_name) as edge_cluster by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | where match(lower(coalesce(request_uri,resource_path,_raw)),"(?i)tier-0|edge-cluster|gateway|bgp|nat|vpn") | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(target_transport_node,resource_id,""))) | stats latest(_time) as audit_ts latest(user_name) as audit_actor by edge_key ]
| join type=left edge_key [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-6h@m latest=now | where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|evacuat|vmotion") | eval edge_key=trim(lower(coalesce(host_mo_id,host_id,""))) | stats latest(_time) as vc_ts max(if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance"),1,0)) as vc_mm by edge_key ]
| eval severity=case(fault_class="gateway_health_not_green",95,fault_class="bgp_not_established_like",90,fault_class="vpn_service_stressed",85,fault_class="lb_service_stressed",80,fault_class="nat_service_stressed",75,1==1,50)
| eval suppress=if(tn_mm==1 OR vc_mm==1 OR audit_ts>relative_time(last_seen,"-12m"),1,0)
| where suppress==0
| sort - severity - last_seen
| head 200
| table last_seen severity edge_key lr_latest fault_class health_latest bgp_latest nat_latest lb_latest vpn_latest edge_cluster audit_ts audit_actor vc_ts
```

## Visualization

(1) **Matrix** — `edge_key` × `fault_class` with **`severity`** hues. (2) **Single value** — counts per **`gateway_health_not_green`**/**`bgp_not_established_like`**. (3) **Timeline** — `last_seen` for SR regressions. (4) **Table** — `health_latest`, `bgp_latest`, `nat_latest`, `lb_latest`, `vpn_latest`, `audit_actor`. (5) **Detail** — `vc_ts` + `audit_ts` for change correlation.

## Known False Positives

**policy_realization_lag** — Policy UI badges can lag HTTPS JSON for minutes while NSX Manager VIP members roll; Splunk shows DOWN labels that consoles later soften once realization catches up. Compare the same `{id}` in CLI service-router output and wait two poll cycles before treating as hard down.

**planned_sr_failover_drill** — Controlled Tier-0 failover exercises intentionally trip `bgp_not_established_like` rows even though the WAN playbook demands a brief non-Established window. Gate alerts with `nsx_edge_change_window.csv` CRQ IDs and require operator acknowledgement fields in tickets.

**api_partial_state_page** — REST workers that forget `cursor` pagination emit only the first page of SR service health; `nat_latest`/`lb_latest` look empty while SSH still shows healthy tables. Fix collectors to honor `result_count` and page until complete before notifying.

**graceful_restart_bgp_hold** — BGP graceful restart can hold peers in softer states while data plane forwarding continues; `bgp_raw` may not read Established yet forwarding is fine per carrier GR policies. Cross-check NSX BGP detailed status and carrier maintenance bulletins.

**single_uplink_lab_noise** — Engineering sandboxes single-home Edge VMs on purpose while testing firewalls; `gateway_health_not_green` stays lit for hours. Filter non-production `edge_cluster` tags out of paging macros.

**edge_vmotion_blip** — vMotion of Edge VMs across ESXi hosts restarts datapath services; one poll interval shows `vpn_service_stressed` then clears. Join `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` tasks and suppress when `vc_ts` hugs `last_seen`.

**stale_status_poll_after_vip_failover** — When NSX Manager VIP fails over, in-flight `/transport-nodes/{id}/status` polls occasionally return cached stale JSON from the previous leader; edge_status may contradict fresh gateway_health until the cursor resets. Validate against CLI before executive pages.

**nat_rule_publish_shadow** — Large NAT publish jobs can make transient `nat_service_stressed` rows while DFW and NAT tables reprogram even though sessions forward correctly. Require two failing polls plus absence of matching audit success rows before paging NAT owners.

## References

- [VMware NSX 4.2 API — GET Tier-0 state](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/api-reference.html)
- [VMware NSX 4.2 Administration — Routing and Edge services](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide/about-routing-in-nsx.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX-T (Splunkbase 4856)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 1810)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)
- [Splunk Common Information Model — Network Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/NetworkTraffic)
